title: Protecting Yourself from AI Surveillance
modules: Module 1: How AI-Powered Surveillance Works — Tracking, Profiling, and Inference at Scale
Module 2: Who Is Watching — Advertisers, Data Brokers, Employers, and State Actors
Module 3: What Your Devices Expose — Sensors, Metadata, and Behavioral Fingerprints
Module 4: Practical Countermeasures — Settings, Tools, and Habits That Actually Work
Module 5: When Surveillance Comes From People You Know — Stalkerware and Domestic Threats
Module 6: Your Legal Rights — Data Deletion, Opt-Outs, and When to Escalate
Module 7: Building a Sustainable Privacy Routine Without Paranoia
synopsis: AI has supercharged the ability of corporations, governments, and abusive individuals to track behavior, infer personal facts, and build detailed profiles from data most people assume is harmless. This course gives everyday users clear, actionable steps to reduce their surveillance exposure without requiring technical expertise or abandoning the tools they rely on.
rationale: AI-powered data aggregation and behavioral inference have made traditional privacy advice — clear your cookies, use incognito mode — dangerously insufficient against the current threat landscape.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit their primary devices and accounts for surveillance exposure, apply a layered set of countermeasures appropriate to their personal threat model, and know when to seek additional legal or technical help.
source_signals: Launch HN: Tinfoil (YC X25): Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI
Launch HN: Overwatch (YC S22): OSINT platform for cyber and fraud risk
Cyber Crime से बचना है? ये ज़रूर देखें!
nearest_existing: How AI Systems Consume and Expose Personal Data
